methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $500M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Divergent is a Series E robotics and manufacturing company with a current post-money valuation of $2.3B and $600M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of nine same-sector peers spanning similar valuation tiers, comprising eight private companies (including 1X Technologies at $10.0B, Foundation Robotics at $3.0B, Exotec at $2.0B, Locus Robotics at $2.0B, and Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B) and one public company. The peer range spans $500M to $10.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $3.2B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set produces a $1.8B median implied value, with the full peer range of $500M to $10.0B reflecting material heterogeneity in stage and business model within robotics. 

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $1.5B (low) to $3.7B (high), with a median of $2.5B. Divergent's current $2.3B valuation sits within this band, near the lower-median position. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—reflecting stage, funding efficiency, and business-model variation—and the limited applicability of the single public comparable to a pre-revenue or early-revenue private company.
